Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Category Theory Illustrated – Types 51 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Show HN: TurboQuant for vector search – 2-4 bit compression 38 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Go on Embedded Systems and WebAssembly 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Show HN: Apfel – The free AI already on your Mac 556 upvotes, 125 comments
  5. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1687 upvotes, 446 comments
  6. Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs 460 upvotes, 134 comments
  7. iNaturalist 85 upvotes, 19 comments
  8. A Recipe for Steganogravy 97 upvotes, 23 comments
  9. What we learned building 100 API integrations with OpenCode 71 upvotes, 16 comments
  10. What Category Theory Teaches Us About DataFrames 147 upvotes, 45 comments
  11. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 29 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. Intel Assured Supply Chain Product Brief 44 upvotes, 10 comments
  13. Solana Drift Protocol drained of $285M via fake token and governance hijack 45 upvotes, 11 comments
  14. April 2026 TLDR Setup for Ollama and Gemma 4 26B on a Mac mini 230 upvotes, 94 comments
  15. SSH certificates: the better SSH experience 142 upvotes, 55 comments
  16. NHS staff refusing to use FDP over Palantir ethical concerns 261 upvotes, 111 comments
  17. Tailscale's new macOS home 532 upvotes, 286 comments
  18. Samsung Magician disk utility takes 18 steps and two reboots to uninstall 262 upvotes, 141 comments
  19. If you're running OpenClaw, you probably got hacked in the last week 137 upvotes, 70 comments
  20. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 23 upvotes, 7 comments
  21. ESP32-S31: Dual-Core RISC-V SoC with Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.4, and Advanced HMI 163 upvotes, 91 comments
  22. Critics say EU risks ceding control of its tech laws under U.S. pressure 190 upvotes, 122 comments
  23. Cursor 3 517 upvotes, 379 comments
  24. TDF ejects its core developers 123 upvotes, 80 comments
  25. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 40 upvotes, 22 comments
  26. Mercurial Dyson – a plan for the disassembly of planet Mercury 27 upvotes, 15 comments
  27. Understanding young news audiences at a time of rapid change 25 upvotes, 14 comments
  28. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 64 upvotes, 48 comments
  29. Solar and batteries can power the world 213 upvotes, 304 comments
  30. Why the heck are we still using Markdown? 18 upvotes, 19 comments

Last generated 2:30am, 4 April 2026. Source here.
